files_to_change.change: maxFileIDInCatalog: skip page IDs not marked used in the free-list bitmap (fm.isUsed) before calling fm.ReadPage.
Add regression test freeing a high-numbered page with a stale high-FileID record and asserting maxFileIDInCatalog ignores it.

behavior_change_risk: Low. Today no code path calls FileManager.FreePage on catalog pages, so highest==used for every existing production/test scenario and the new isUsed check is a no-op in all current call sites. Only the new regression test exercises the changed branch.
concurrency_considerations: fm.isUsed is not internally synchronized (matches AllocatePage/FreePage convention of calling it under fm.mu). Fix takes fm.mu.Lock() once to snapshot which page IDs are used, matching file.go's documented locking model of not holding mu across page I/O (ReadPage/WritePage do their own separate synchronization).

test_impact.existing: go test ./engine/catalog/... -run TestMaxFileIDInCatalog -v must continue to pass unchanged.
test_impact.new: New subtest/case in idalloc_test.go exercising a freed high-numbered page.

docs_impact: docs/LLD/catalog.md's maxFileIDInCatalog description already implies 'currently-allocated' pages only; no wording change strictly required, but the doc's list of gaps/known-issues (if any references this regression id) should be checked for closure bookkeeping.
